calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-17-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HERBERT WONG'S MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT JUDGMENT.

Defendant Herbert Wong's motion to set aside the judgment entered against him on July 14, 1989 is denied. Defendant appears to be objecting to the February 26, 2019 order extending the period of enforceability of that judgment by 10 years, but he has not presented any factual basis for his claim that the judgment lien was imposed against him as a result of fraud or misconduct by Plaintiffs' counsel, and has not shown any other grounds for such relief. While equitable relief from a default judgment can be sought at any time on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake, it does not appear that the judgment in question was a default judgment; rather, it apparently was entered after a jury trial. In any event, Defendant has not shown any of the essential requirements to obtain such relief, including a meritorious defense, a satisfactory excuse for not presenting a defense to the original action, or diligence in seeking to set aside the default once it was discovered. (E.g., Rappleyea v. Campbell (1994) 8 Cal.4th 975, 982.) Defendant admits that he "discovered" the judgment when he received the recorded judgment lien in August 1989-nearly 30 years ago. Further, he previously filed two motions to vacate the judgment, both of which were denied.

A proposed order is submitted by email.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
